elements.title: Michael Gove
elements.contents: ... that Michael Gove (pictured), now a member of the House of Lords, appeared on the television quiz show Top Club?

elements.title: Hippocampus
elements.contents: ... that the loss of the freighter Hippocampus represented the deadliest disaster in the southwestern Michigan fruit trade?

elements.title: Gwen Wakeling
elements.contents: ... that costume designer Gwen Wakeling crafted Shirley Temple's on-screen image by working on 14 of her films?

elements.title: Fly-Day Chinatown
elements.contents: ... that the melody of "Fly-Day Chinatown" was said to have "the soaring wings of a phoenix"?

elements.title: Kathleen Weil-Garris Brandt
elements.contents: ... that Kathleen Weil-Garris Brandt once attributed a New York City statue to Michelangelo?

elements.title: one of Indonesia's largest charities
elements.contents: ... that one of Indonesia's largest charities was established by a group of journalists in response to a local famine?

elements.title: Francis Scobell
elements.contents: ... that Francis Scobell prevented Charles Mohun, 4th Baron Mohun from killing a coachman?

elements.title: one Legends of Tomorrow episode
elements.contents: ... that one Legends of Tomorrow episode is named after a classic Western film?

elements.title: Rafael Polinario
elements.contents: ... that Cuban swimmer Rafael Polinario had to defect to Canada instead of Spain after Fidel Castro's brother got on his plane?
